intro: Professionals searching for account currency vs ledger currency are usually separating account-level custody from entity-level measurement. Account currency governs how balances are assigned and reconciled at State 04; ledger currency hosts the trial balance and measurement shell at State 05. The distinction matters for ERP close design, revaluation populations, subledger mapping, and audit reconstruction.
doctrine_statement: Account currency controls balance custody. Ledger currency hosts measured books.
primary_section_body: Account currency is granular: it attaches currency identity to account populations and reconciliations.
Ledger currency is structural: it hosts entity books, trial balance integrity, and measurement continuity.
The two layers interact during close, but a chart-level custody setting is not the same as the measurement currency of the ledger.

pillar_sections.title: Side-by-side distinction
paragraphs: Use this section as the quick comparison layer: what each term means, where it sits in the currency-state chain, and which evidence proves it.

steps.title: Account currency
steps.body: State 04. Currency identity assigned to balances, accounts, and reconciliation populations.

steps.title: Ledger currency
steps.body: State 05. The measurement shell where postings, subledger rolls, revaluation, and trial balance closure are maintained.

pillar_sections.title: Operational consequences of the distinction
paragraphs: ERP consequence: account master data, posting logic, and ledger settings may each hold different parts of the truth.
Close consequence: revaluation batches can be wrong if account-currency populations are assumed from ledger currency alone.
Reconciliation consequence: a trial balance can tie while account-level foreign populations remain poorly explained.
Reporting consequence: ledger totals may obscure the account-currency custody decisions that shaped FX outcomes.

pillar_sections.title: Where confusion creates risk
paragraphs: Confusion creates risk when teams use local currency, functional currency, ledger currency, and account currency interchangeably.
The failure pattern is a clean trial balance that cannot be replayed back to controlled account populations.

pillar_sections.title: Questions each concept must answer
paragraphs: These questions are reference prompts for evidence discipline. They are not audit, accounting, tax, legal, treasury, or ERP implementation advice.
questions: Account currency custody question: which account population proves the maintained currency of this balance?
Ledger currency custody question: which ledger measurement shell proves trial balance completeness?
Bridge question: do revaluation and close procedures explicitly map account populations into ledger measurement?

pillar_sections.title: Glossary, state, and methodology links
paragraphs: The related references keep the comparison inside AccountCcy.com terminology and make the trust boundary explicit. The methodology link explains claim limits and source discipline.

pillar_sections.title: Practical comparison questions
questions: Is ledger currency just another name for account currency? No. Account currency is account-level custody; ledger currency is the measurement shell for books.
Which one drives the trial balance? Ledger currency hosts trial balance measurement, while account currency shapes the populations feeding it.
Why does this matter for revaluation? Revaluation depends on controlled populations, not only ledger-level labels.

graph_title: Keep comparison intent inside the custody chain
graph_intro: The purpose of this page is to strengthen professional search intent while preserving AccountCcy.com claim discipline and state architecture.

cards.label: State position
cards.title: Each term belongs to a different control layer.
cards.body: The distinction is strongest when mapped to the CCY State Chain rather than reduced to screen labels.

cards.label: Evidence
cards.title: Different evidence proves different currency identities.
cards.body: Configuration, payment, ledger, translation, and consolidation artifacts should not be substituted for each other.

cards.label: Trust boundary
cards.title: The page is a reference framework, not advice.
cards.body: The methodology page defines claim limits, source boundaries, and publication discipline.

control_title: Map account populations into ledger measurement
control_body: Document how account-currency populations roll into ledger currency close routines, including subledger feeds, posting logic, and revaluation runs.
risk_title: Clean ledger totals without custody replay
risk_body: The professional risk is not only imbalance; it is the inability to prove which account-currency populations produced the measured ledger result.
